# Climate / Envelope — Juplaya Trailer Build

Split from `../juplaya-trailer-context.md` (doc divorce, design-panel run).

## Settled
- **Closed-cell spray foam** insulation — given.
- **Elastomeric roof coating** — given.
- **Velit Mini 2000R AC — ordered (48 VDC unit).** Runs on the 48 V battery bus with its own correctly rated fuse/breaker (see [power](power.md)).

## Windows
- **One** framed curbside awning window sized to the **measured** clear opening (≤ ~26" RO), spanning two 16"-OC bays with a load-transfer sub-frame; fallback = no glass + a powered roof-vent fan. (agy falsified "two ~22\" windows" — bays leave ~15" clear.) **Gate: measured RO before ordering.**

## HRV
- Defer the core; **rough-in the paired 4" wall penetrations + power string now.** (A future HRV is a 24 V-friendly pick.)

## Diesel heater
- **No heater for July**; rough-in through-floor exhaust/fuel penetrations + a hardwired CO-detector string now; defer the unit to cold-weather profiles. (agy killed "install now": mild July nights vs under-floor-exhaust fire over dry scrub + nose-heavy fuel.) **A future heater is bought as a 24 V model — and reopens converter sizing (D006 condition).**

## Awning — DECIDED: Fiamma F45s 350, riser-mounted (D007)
- **Unit:** Fiamma **F45s 350** (Polar White / Royal Grey, 06280B01R) — 11'6" case, 10'10" canopy, 8'2" extension, ~55 lb. The July deliverable; order long-lead now, with **Tie Down S (98655-133)** + 3/8"×12" lag anchors + deadman bags.
- **Mount:** owner-fabricated **steel riser brackets** at the curbside roof edge / top plate, holding the case **at or above the roofline (case bottom ≥ ~78.5")**. Wall-mount is dead (case 5.35" vs ~4.5" clear above the 72" door; deployed fabric fouls the swing). Flat-roof-adapter mount is dead (the panels own the roof width).
- **Load path (binding, panel review):** brackets land on/into the **steel tube posts** (76½" tube posts, 16" OC per the work order) with interior backing plates — not rail-only; design for **deployed wind + tie-down loads and travel vibration** (the 138" case is a long lever), not the static 55 lb.
- **Required engineering outputs before fabrication:** bracket **count & spacing** along the 138" case; **fastener size/grade**; **backing-plate dimensions spanning the studs/top plate**; documented load case = deployed wind + tie-down tension + travel vibration.
- **Pass/fail gates BEFORE fabrication:** (a) door-trim height above the 72" cutout; (b) roofline/rail/top-plate section + backing at the curb edge; (c) the **measured roof drawing** shared with solar (see [power](power.md)); (d) **deployed-fabric vs open-door at actual pitch** (margin is ~0.9" on paper — thin).
- **Fallback if gates fail:** freestanding/portable shade (Moonshade-class) anchored to trailer + ground — zero trailer structure.
- **Discipline:** legs to ground (never wall-foot); wound in before sleep, departure, or gusts >20 mph. It's shade, not a storm shelter.

## Measurement gates (climate)
- Window RO; door-trim height; roofline/top-plate section; deployed-fabric/door check.
